summary: Mexican novelist, part of the Latin American Boom. Known for The Death of Artemio Cruz, Aura, Terra Nostra, The Old Gringo, and Where the Air is Clear. Friend of Gabriel García Márquez and part of the 'Boom' generation.

works.name: The Death of Artemio Cruz
works.description: Novel using three narrative voices (I, you, he) to tell the story of a dying Mexican Revolution veteran.

clues.clue: Three narrative voices: first person (I), second person (you/tú), third person (he); dying Mexican Revolution veteran Artemio Cruz recalls his life
examples: told from three alternating perspectives: first, second, and third person

clues.clue: Cruz betrays his revolutionary ideals and becomes a corrupt businessman; dies on the date April 10, 1959
examples: a dying former revolutionary turned corrupt businessman

works.name: Aura
works.description: Novella written in second person about a young historian drawn into an old woman's household.

clues.clue: Written entirely in second-person 'you' (tú); young historian Felipe Montero catalogues memoirs for elderly widow Consuelo; Aura is Consuelo's young double
examples: Felipe Montero is hired to catalogue memoirs

works.name: Terra Nostra
works.description: Massive novel spanning from the Roman era to the year 2000.

clues.clue: Massive novel spanning from Roman times to 1999; features Philip II and El Escorial

examples: part of the Latin American Boom

clues.clue: The Old Gringo — American journalist Ambrose Bierce disappears in the Mexican Revolution

comprehensive_summary:
Carlos Fuentes (1928-2012) was a Mexican novelist and a key figure in the Latin American literary Boom alongside García Márquez, Vargas Llosa, and Cortázar.

The Death of Artemio Cruz uses three alternating narrative voices — first person (I), second person (you/tú), and third person (he) — to tell the story of the dying Mexican Revolution veteran Artemio Cruz, who betrayed his revolutionary ideals to become corrupt. Aura is a novella written entirely in second person, in which the young historian Felipe Montero is hired by elderly widow Consuelo to catalogue memoirs, and discovers that the mysterious young Aura is Consuelo's double. Terra Nostra is a massive novel spanning from the Roman era to 1999. The Old Gringo is loosely based on Ambrose Bierce's disappearance in Mexico.
